title: isolepis dichotoma
intro: Isolepis dichotoma, also known as the cotton-grass or woolly heads, is a type of plant that belongs to the sedge family and is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Isolepis dichotoma include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Isolepis dichotoma can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported healing properties.
